entry.title: Are Claude Code Skills Safe? How to Vet an AI Skill Before You Install It
entry.content: AI skills run with your permissions and pull in real dependencies. Here is a practical 7-point security checklist for vetting any Claude Code skill, MCP server, or SKILL.md repo before you install it.

entry.title: What is an AI Skills Marketplace? A Complete Guide for 2026
entry.content: An AI skills marketplace is a curated directory of reusable capabilities for AI coding assistants. Definitions, how they work, and how to choose between them in 2026.

entry.title: Best MCP Servers in 2026: Complete Directory by Category
entry.content: A category-by-category guide to the best Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in 2026. Databases, dev tools, productivity, search, browser automation - what to install for Claude Code, Codex, and ChatGPT.

entry.title: Caveman: The Claude Code Skill That Cuts Token Use by 65%
entry.content: How Caveman compresses Claude's responses to caveman-speak, the four modes from Lite to Classical Chinese, and what it teaches us about prompt design when token bills matter.

entry.title: SkillsLLM Features Guide: Discover, Compare, and Track AI Skills
entry.content: A complete walkthrough of everything you can do on SkillsLLM - browsing, voting, bookmarking, comparing skills side-by-side, and finding what other developers like.

entry.title: The Zettelkasten Method in Obsidian: A Practical Setup Guide
entry.content: Learn how to set up a working Zettelkasten in Obsidian: note types, vault structure, MOCs, and how AI handles the maintenance that kills most systems.

entry.title: How to Bulk Rename Obsidian Files Without Breaking Every Link
entry.content: Learn how to bulk rename Obsidian files without breaking wikilinks. Compare the Bulk Rename plugin, VS Code workarounds, and AI-powered renaming.

entry.title: Obsidian Find and Replace Across All Files: How to Make it Work
entry.content: Learn how to find and replace text across all files in your Obsidian vault using built-in tools, plugins, VS Code, and Desktop Commander AI.

entry.title: Notion MCP Server: How to Give Your AI Full Access to Your Workspace
entry.content: Set up Notion MCP to give Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor direct access to your Notion workspace. Covers hosted and open-source options, 17 tools, and troubleshoo...

entry.title: The Personal Management System Guide: What Works, What Fails, and How to Build Your Own
entry.content: Build a personal management system that works across all your files and tools using AI-powered local file access — no app switching required.

entry.title: Knowledge Management System: Why Most Fail and What Actually Works
entry.content: Most knowledge management systems fail from maintenance overhead. Use an AI-native local approach to retrieve answers from your existing files.

entry.title: 14 Best Obsidian Plugins in 2026: Must-Haves, Nice-to-Haves, and Power Tools
entry.content: Discover the 14 best Obsidian plugins in 2026 for beginners to power users. Clear verdicts, use-cases, and tips for each plugin.

entry.title: Knowledge Management System: Why Most Fail and What Actually Works
entry.content: Most knowledge management systems fail because they demand constant upkeep. Learn how AI-native local tools like Desktop Commander turn your existing files i...

entry.title: I’m biased, but here’s my comparison of Desktop Commander vs Cowork
entry.content: An honest comparison of Desktop Commander and Claude Cowork from someone on the DC team. See where each tool excels — terminal access, file editing, deployme...

entry.title: Markdown to PDF: An Approach to Sharing Your Documents
entry.content: Convert Markdown to styled PDF with custom formatting, headers, and code blocks. Compare CLI tools, VS Code extensions, and AI-powered conversion methods.

entry.title: Best MCP Servers for Knowledge Bases in 2026
entry.content: Compare the best MCP servers for building AI-powered knowledge bases in 2026. From Notion and Obsidian to vector search and knowledge graphs.

entry.title: Obsidian Markdown Cheatsheet: Every Syntax You Actually Need
entry.content: Complete Obsidian Markdown cheatsheet with formatting, links, callouts, embeds, and tables. Copy-paste examples for all the syntax you'll use daily.

entry.title: How to Use Tags in Obsidian Markdown
entry.content: Learn how to use tags in Obsidian markdown effectively. Master nested tags, best practices, and discover how AI can automate tag management across your vault.

entry.title: I Can Tell When You’re Using AI in My Interviews. Here’s How.
entry.content: A hiring manager shares how to spot candidates using real-time AI during interviews — and why saying I dont know is now the most impressive answer.

entry.title: Markdown Tables: Complete Syntax Guide
entry.content: Master Markdown table syntax with this complete guide. Learn alignment, formatting, and tools to create perfect tables every time.

entry.title: Supabase MCP: How to Connect AI to Your Database
entry.content: Learn how to connect Claude and Cursor to your Supabase database using MCP. Complete setup guide with security best practices and real SQL examples.

entry.title: Claude and MCPs: How to Use MCP with Claude AI
entry.content: Learn how to use Claude MCP (Model Context Protocol) to connect Claude AI to your files, terminal, and tools. Complete setup guide with practical examples.

entry.title: Create and Manage Your Obsidian Knowledge Base with AI
entry.content: Learn how to manage your Obsidian vault with AI using Desktop Commander. Organize attachments, rename notes, and clean up your knowledge base with natural la...

entry.title: Markdown File: The Complete Reference Guide for 2026 (with cheatsheet)
entry.content: Markdown files are plain text documents with simple formatting. Learn why .md files are the foundation for AI-powered file management and modern documentatio...

entry.title: Build a Personal AI Knowledge Base with Local Files
entry.content: Learn how to build a personal AI knowledge base using local files. Skip the complex RAG pipelines—manage your notes and documents with natural language throu...

entry.title: Automate Docker Workflows with AI: From Container Logs to Compose Files
entry.content: Use AI to manage Docker containers, debug errors, and automate compose workflows locally. No memorizing commands—describe what you need in plain English.

entry.title: Technical Documentation with Markdown: Best Practices & Tools
entry.content: Master markdown best practices for technical documentation. Learn formatting, version control, error checking tools, and how Desktop Commander automates docu...

entry.title: AI Code Review with Desktop Commander: A Practical Guide
entry.content: Learn how to review code with AI using Desktop Commander. Analyze codebases, find bugs, and get recommendations through natural language—no complex setup.

entry.title: How to Read and Process CSV Files in Python: Complete Guide
entry.content: Learn how to read and process CSV files using the built-in Python csv module and Pandas. Discover how Desktop Commander can automate the entire workflow.

entry.title: The 22 best MCP servers (2025 and 2026)
entry.content: Discover the 22 best MCP servers for Claude, Cursor, and AI coding assistants. Complete guide with installation, features, and real use cases for 2025-2026.

entry.title: What is an MCP server and how it works (in plain English)
entry.content: Discover what an MCP server is, how it works, and best MCP servers and clients you should use in 2025/2026.

entry.title: How to Set Up Local Development Environment with Ease
entry.content: Learn how to automatically set up local development environment with an MCP server using natural language prompts and how it solves common challenges.

entry.title: How to Create a SKILL.md File
entry.content: A complete guide to writing effective SKILL.md files that help AI assistants understand and use your tools.

entry.title: Getting Started with AI Skills for Claude Code
entry.content: Learn how to discover, install, and use AI skills to supercharge your development workflow with Claude Code and other AI assistants.

entry.title: Contact
entry.content: Get in touch with the Desktop Commander team — join our Discord community, open a GitHub issue, or follow us on X for updates.
